prompt: Flask currently only exposes MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH as a per-application config key, and even then the Request.max_content_length property can only be read, not overridden per-request. Werkzeug also provides two other form-parsing limits — max_form_memory_size (maximum size of any non-file multipart field) and max_form_parts (maximum number of multipart fields) — but these have no Flask config equivalents at all. Add MAX_FORM_MEMORY_SIZE (default 500_000) and MAX_FORM_PARTS (default 1_000) as Flask config keys in app.py. Then expose all three limits as settable properties on the Request class in wrappers.py so that individual views can override them for a specific request. Each property must handle three cases in priority order: a per-request instance override if set, the Flask app config value if inside an app context, and otherwise the Werkzeug base class default.
human_annotation_notes: globals.py defines the current_app proxy via LocalProxy. The critical constraint is how current_app behaves outside an app context: `if not current_app` evaluates to True when there is no active application context, at which point the property must fall back to `super().max_form_memory_size` (the Werkzeug default) rather than accessing config. An agent that never reads globals.py will likely write a simpler property that always tries to access current_app.config and raises a RuntimeError outside an app context, or will omit the out-of-context fallback entirely. The three-way priority chain (instance override → config → Werkzeug default) is the core invariant. Testing.py in the CDM output is noise.

assert_design.cpc_description: Solution must: (1) add MAX_FORM_MEMORY_SIZE and MAX_FORM_PARTS defaults to app.py config dict, (2) implement max_form_memory_size and max_form_parts as properties with setters on Request, (3) check for per-request instance override first, (4) check app context via current_app before accessing config, (5) fall back to super() when outside app context

constraint_propagation_targets.description: Config key must be added to Flask default config in app.py
max_form_memory_size and max_form_parts must be implemented as properties
Properties must check current_app to distinguish in-context from out-of-context
Must fall back to Werkzeug base class defaults when outside app context
